$note: Work-type pipelines (declarative). The orchestrator reads this: parse `sdlc <type> <slug>` (default type=feature), then run the type's `phases` IN ORDER, using each phase's agent + gate from the shared catalogs below. Phases, gates, and OpenSpec lifecycle are defined ONCE (reused by every type); each type only references them + sets delta mode and overrides. Edit per project to tune which phases a type runs.

lifecycle.implement: /opsx:apply   (or the openspec-apply skill — loops tasks.md checkboxes)
lifecycle.archive: openspec archive "<change-name>"   (merges spec deltas into openspec/specs/)

S2.produces: AC scenarios + Early Risk Flags + stride

gateCatalog.SPEC_LOCK: spec-auditor PASS  AND  openspec change validate passes
gateCatalog.DESIGN_REVIEW: cross-artifact-audit 0 CRITICAL  AND  openspec change validate passes
gateCatalog.QA: 0 Critical/High bugs open; all ACs verified
gateCatalog.RELEASE: migration reviewed + rollback plan + 30-min post-deploy stable

rebuild.desc: Re-implement existing behavior. Like feature, but read the existing source for parity first.

cr.desc: Change request to an existing capability. Spec delta uses MODIFIED requirements.
cr.note: S3 only if the change alters design; otherwise skip straight to S4.

bugfix.desc: Fast-track: clear root cause, no design change. Skip S1-S3.

gateOverrides.S5: regression-only (retest + regression scope; no full test design)

bugfix.note: Still `openspec new change` with a short proposal + a spec delta ONLY if behavior changes. Escalate to full feature if scope grows.

hotfix.desc: Emergency production fix. Minimal build + smoke test, deploy fast.

gateOverrides.S4: minimal — fix + a regression test for the incident only

hotfix.note: Branch `hotfix/<ticket>-<slug>`. Backfill spec delta + S5 retroactively after the incident if behavior changed.
